+{% set name = "sunkit-magex" %}
+{% set version = "1.0.0a1" %}
+
+package:
+  name: {{ name|lower }}
+  version: {{ version }}
+
+source:
+  url: https://pypi.io/packages/source/{{ name[0] }}/{{ name }}/sunkit_magex-{{ version }}.tar.gz
+  sha256: 606199962cef4d5c89c37a924caaf984314f8625999d84b0c95c3c3ee9fc9820
+
+build:
+  noarch: python
+  script: {{ PYTHON }} -m pip install . -vv --no-deps --no-build-isolation
+  number: 0
+
+requirements:
+  host:
+    - python >=3.10
+    - setuptools >=62.1
+    - setuptools-scm >=6.2
+    - wheel
+    - pip
+  run:
+    - python >=3.10
+    - astropy >=5.2.0
+    - numpy >=1.23.0
+    - scikit-image >=0.19.0
+    - scipy >=1.8.0,!=1.10.0
+    - sunpy >=5.1
+    - streamtracer >=1.0.0
+
+test:
+  imports:
+    - sunkit_magex
+  commands:
+    - pip check
+    - pytest --doctest-rst -p no:unraisableexception -p no:threadexception --arraydiff
+  requires:
+    - pip
+    - pytest
+    - pytest-doctestplus
+    - pytest-arraydiff
+    - parfive
+    - reproject
+    - streamtracer
+    - sympy
+
+about:
+  summary: Solar Magnetic field Extrapolation
+  license: GPL-3.0
+  license_file: licenses/LICENSE.rst
+
+extra:
+  recipe-maintainers:
+    - | --------------- | ----------------------------- |

| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [ ] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Currently, the user has to manually define the tracer to use with, e.g., `tracer = pfss.tracing.FortranTracer()`, where `FortranTracer` requires the (optional) `streamtracer` package; the built-in alternative is `PythonTracer`. I'd suggest to add a general function `pfss.tracing.Tracer()` that tries to load the more performant tracer (Fortran/rust), and if it fails, falls back to the Python one (maybe giving a warning/info).

This would be obsolete if streamtracer were a non-optional a dependency.


### Proposed solution

So this doc references `sunkit-magex.pfss.tracing.FortranTracer` which still exists. My understanding is if you have streamtracer <v1.2.0 then using this class will use Fortran, but otherwise FotranTracer in fact uses RUST - is that true?

That's also my understanding.

> If so (if the version affects the tracing backend language) - then should we rename the class PerfomanceTracer or something similar? or if Fortran support is going away entirely, rename it RustTracer ?

That's why I added "Automatically pick working/best Tracer" #37 :sweat_smile: But yeah, I think the idea is to get rid of Fortran totally, and then I would give it a generic name.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7565","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7565/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7565/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7565/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7565","id":2233725533,"node_id":"I_kwDOACEKh86FI_Jd","number":7565,"title":"read_cdf/TimeSeries crashes if CDF variable doesn't have a 'FILLVAL' attribute","user":{"login":"jgieseler","id":39489154,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5NDg5MTU0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39489154?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler","html_url":"https://github.com/jgieseler","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-04-09T15:29:46Z","updated_at":"2024-04-09T15:29:46Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the bug

Similar to https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/5907, I stumbled upon CDF files that aren't fully [complying to the CDF ISTP Metadata Guidelines](https://spdf.gsfc.nasa.gov/sp_use_of_cdf.html) by having a variable that doesn't provide a corresponding FILLVAL attribute. `read_cdf()` is expecting this attribute to exist, and otherwise crashes (and with it also `TimeSeries`):
https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/9f0a37dc0d0a543663e871a3256b147fc004db2e/sunpy/io/_cdf.py#L83-L87

But as has been mentioned in https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/5907#issuecomment-1054428488, it's not uncommon that a CDF file is not fully following the ISTP standard (these are by the way recent PSP data files, so I'll anyhow contact the instrument team). If the `FILLVAL` information isn't provided by the instrument team, one could just skip the replacement call. So I propose to change `if np.issubdtype(data.dtype, np.floating):` to something like `if np.issubdtype(data.dtype, np.floating) and 'FILLVAL' in attrs:`

### To Reproduce

Because the error message occurs in `read_cdf` when using `TimeSeries`, this is what I'm calling here directly:

``` python
import datetime as dt
from sunpy.io._cdf import read_cdf
from sunpy.net import Fido
from sunpy.net import attrs as a
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries

dataset = 'PSP_SWP_SPI_SF00_L3_MOM'
trange = a.Time(dt.date(2023,3,14), dt.date(2023,3,15))
result = Fido.search(trange, a.cdaweb.Dataset(dataset))
downloaded_files = Fido.fetch(result)
# ts = TimeSeries(downloaded_files, concatenate=True)
data = read_cdf(downloaded_files.data[0])
```

### Screenshots

_No response_

### System Details

- sunpy version: 5.1.1 and 5.1.2

---

### [Rendered Version of the SEP](https://github.com/Cadair/sunpy-SEP/blob/new_sep_2_final/SEP-0002.md)

---

This PR is the final version of #62 where most of the discussion has taken place.

The goal of this PR (which actually modifies SEP 2 in the repo) is to be a final comment period, **we are not expecting to make any substantive changes** during this review. The original PR has been open for *more* than long enough!

The timeline for the adoption of this proposal is as follows:

* 2024/04/09 - This PR opened, final comment period begins
* 2024/04/26 - Final comment period closes
* (no later than) 2024/05/17 - Board meeting merges PR
* 2024/05/20 - Invitations to the initial group of Project Custodians sent out.
* 2024/05/20 - Nominations for first Project Custodian election open.
* 2024/05/27 - Announcement of Steering Committee Election, and Nominations for Steering Committee open.
* 2024/05/27 - Election of Project Custodians announced to initial project custodians
* 2024/06/07 - Election of Project Custodians starts.
* 2024/06/10 - Election of Project Custodians finishes.
* 2024/06/10 - Nomination period for Steering Committee Election finishes.
* 2024/06/17 - Election of new Steering Committee starts.
* 2024/07/01 - Election of new Steering Committee finishes.
* 2024/07/08 - New Steering committee is announced and starts work.

---

### [Rendered Version of the SEP](https://github.com/Cadair/sunpy-SEP/blob/new_sep_2_final/SEP-0002.md)

---

This PR is the final version of #62 where most of the discussion has taken place.

The goal of this PR (which actually modifies SEP 2 in the repo) is to be a final comment period, **we are not expecting to make any substantive changes** during this review. The original PR has been open for *more* than long enough!

The timeline for the adoption of this proposal is as follows:

* 2024/04/09 - This PR opened, final comment period begins
* 2024/04/26 - Final comment period closes
* (no later than) 2024/05/17 - Board meeting merges PR
* 2024/05/20 - Invitations to the initial group of Project Custodians sent out.
* 2024/05/20 - Nominations for first Project Custodian election open.
* 2024/05/27 - Announcement of Steering Committee Election, and Nominations for Steering Committee open.
* 2024/05/27 - Election of Project Custodians announced to initial project custodians
* 2024/06/07 - Election of Project Custodians starts.
* 2024/06/10 - Election of Project Custodians finishes.
* 2024/06/10 - Nomination period for Steering Committee Election finishes.
* 2024/06/17 - Election of new Steering Committee starts.
* 2024/07/01 - Election of new Steering Committee finishes.
* 2024/07/08 - New Steering committee is announced and starts work.

Package Details

- Package name: solarmach
- Maintainer(s): Jan Gieseler
- Repository: https://github.com/jgieseler/solarmach
- Documentation link: https://solarmach.readthedocs.io
- PyPI link (if applicable): https://pypi.org/project/solarmach
- Continuous integration link(s): https://github.com/jgieseler/solarmach/blob/main/.github/workflows/pytest.yml
- Link to Code of Conduct or page describing adopted Code of Conduct: https://github.com/jgieseler/solarmach/blob/main/code_of_conduct.md

## Description of Package
<!-- Roughly 1-3 sentences in length !-->
The Solar MAgnetic Connection HAUS tool (`solarmach`) is a Python package that derives (using `sunpy`) and visualizes the spatial configuration and solar magnetic connection of different observers in the heliosphere at different times. 

(Note: `solarmach` is the PyPI/conda-forge Python package that provides all functionalities to end users and to the `streamlit` web-app at https://solar-mach.github.io. The `streamlit` app itself is also open-source (cf. https://github.com/jgieseler/Solar-MACH), but not provided as a Python package.)


<!-- Submitters do not need to edit below this comment !-->

## Package Review

## Editor Submission Checklist

- [x] Is the submission appropriate (compatible with the SunPy CoC)?
- [x] Does the project have an [appropriate license](https://opensource.org/licenses/)?
- [x] Is the project in an online version control system?
- [x] Does the project provide a Python interface?
- [x] Is the project on PyPI?
- [x] Is the project useful to the solar physics community?
- [x] Version that was reviewed (tag or commit hash) - v0.2.2

## Instructions to Reviewer

Please copy the following and select the ranking for each criteria, the full review criteria can be found here:

```
* Functionality           : General Package / Specialized Package / Not Relevant
* Integration             : Well integrated / Partially Integrated / Minimal Integration
* Documentation           : Extensive / Some / Little
* Testing                 : Excellent / Good / Needs Work
* Duplication             : None / Some / Major
* Community               : Excellent / Good / Needs Work
* Development Status      : Stable / Subject to Change / Low Activity / Needs Work
```

Package Details

- Package name: solarmach
- Maintainer(s): Jan Gieseler
- Repository: https://github.com/jgieseler/solarmach
- Documentation link: https://solarmach.readthedocs.io
- PyPI link (if applicable): https://pypi.org/project/solarmach
- Continuous integration link(s): https://github.com/jgieseler/solarmach/blob/main/.github/workflows/pytest.yml
- Link to Code of Conduct or page describing adopted Code of Conduct: https://github.com/jgieseler/solarmach/blob/main/code_of_conduct.md

## Description of Package
<!-- Roughly 1-3 sentences in length !-->
The Solar MAgnetic Connection HAUS tool (`solarmach`) is a Python package that derives (using `sunpy`) and visualizes the spatial configuration and solar magnetic connection of different observers in the heliosphere at different times. 

(Note: `solarmach` is the PyPI/conda-forge Python package that provides all functionalities to end users and to the `streamlit` web-app at https://solar-mach.github.io. The `streamlit` app itself is also open-source (cf. https://github.com/jgieseler/Solar-MACH), but not provided as a Python package.)


<!-- Submitters do not need to edit below this comment !-->

Probably originating in https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7204, when loading a GONG map with sunpy.map.Map with sunpy 5.1.0rc, a MultipleMatchError is raised: 

> MultipleMatchError: Too many candidate types identified ([<class 'sunpy.map.sources.gong.GONGSynopticMap'>, <class 'pfsspy.map.GongSynopticMap'>]). Specify enough keywords to guarantee unique type identification.


### To Reproduce

``` python
>>> import sunpy
>>> from pfsspy.sample_data import get_gong_map
>>> gong_fname = get_gong_map()
>>> gong_map = sunpy.map.Map(gong_fname)
MultipleMatchError: Too many candidate types identified ([<class 'sunpy.map.sources.gong.GONGSynopticMap'>, <class 'pfsspy.map.GongSynopticMap'>]). Specify enough keywords to guarantee unique type identification.
```

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7313","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7313/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7313/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7313/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7313","id":2005911289,"node_id":"I_kwDOACEKh853j8b5","number":7313,"title":"Missing GONG map meta data with sunpy 5.1.0 causes KeyError in pfsspy","user":{"login":"jgieseler","id":39489154,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5NDg5MTU0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39489154?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler","html_url":"https://github.com/jgieseler","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-11-22T09:24:28Z","updated_at":"2023-11-22T09:24:28Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the bug

Probably related to #7204 and #7294, when loading a GONG map with sunpy.map.Map with sunpy 5.1.0, some meta data are missing compared to using sunpy 5.0 and loading the GONG map with pfsspy. This causes pfsspy to crash with a `KeyError` when using its `tracer` function. 

### To Reproduce

In principle, running the [GONG PFSS extrapolation example from the pfsspy docs](https://pfsspy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/auto_examples/using_pfsspy/plot_gong.html). Short version of this:

``` python
import astropy.constants as const
import astropy.units as u
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import sunpy.map
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord

import pfsspy
from pfsspy import coords, tracing
from pfsspy.sample_data import get_gong_map
gong_fname = get_gong_map()
gong_map = sunpy.map.Map(gong_fname)
nrho = 35
rss = 2.5
pfss_in = pfsspy.Input(gong_map, nrho, rss)
pfss_out = pfsspy.pfss(pfss_in)
tracer = tracing.PythonTracer()
r = 1.2 * const.R_sun
lat = np.linspace(-np.pi / 2, np.pi / 2, 8, endpoint=False)
lon = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 8, endpoint=False)
lat, lon = np.meshgrid(lat, lon, indexing='ij')
lat, lon = lat.ravel() * u.rad, lon.ravel() * u.rad
seeds = SkyCoord(lon, lat, r, frame=pfss_out.coordinate_frame)
field_lines = tracer.trace(seeds, pfss_out)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
KeyError                                  Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[9], line 1
----> 1 field_lines = tracer.trace(seeds, pfss_out)

File ~/mambaforge/envs/sunpyrc/lib/python3.9/site-packages/pfsspy/tracing.py:208, in PythonTracer.trace(self, seeds, output)
    206 def trace(self, seeds, output):
    207     self.validate_seeds(seeds)
--> 208     x, y, z = self.coords_to_xyz(seeds, output)
    209     seeds = np.atleast_2d(np.stack((x, y, z), axis=-1))
    211     flines = []

Probably related to #7204 and #7294, when loading a GONG map with sunpy.map.Map with sunpy 5.1.0, some meta data are missing compared to using sunpy 5.0 and loading the GONG map with pfsspy. This causes pfsspy to crash with a `KeyError` when using its `tracer` function. 

### To Reproduce

In principle, running the [GONG PFSS extrapolation example from the pfsspy docs](https://pfsspy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/auto_examples/using_pfsspy/plot_gong.html). Short version of this:

``` python
import astropy.constants as const
import astropy.units as u
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import sunpy.map
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord

import pfsspy
from pfsspy import coords, tracing
from pfsspy.sample_data import get_gong_map
gong_fname = get_gong_map()
gong_map = sunpy.map.Map(gong_fname)
nrho = 35
rss = 2.5
pfss_in = pfsspy.Input(gong_map, nrho, rss)
pfss_out = pfsspy.pfss(pfss_in)
tracer = tracing.PythonTracer()
r = 1.2 * const.R_sun
lat = np.linspace(-np.pi / 2, np.pi / 2, 8, endpoint=False)
lon = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 8, endpoint=False)
lat, lon = np.meshgrid(lat, lon, indexing='ij')
lat, lon = lat.ravel() * u.rad, lon.ravel() * u.rad
seeds = SkyCoord(lon, lat, r, frame=pfss_out.coordinate_frame)
field_lines = tracer.trace(seeds, pfss_out)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Describe the bug

Probably related to #7204 and #7294, when loading a GONG map with sunpy.map.Map with sunpy 5.1.0, some meta data are missing compared to using sunpy 5.0 and loading the GONG map with pfsspy. This causes pfsspy to crash with a `KeyError` when using its `tracer` function. 

### To Reproduce

In principle, running the [GONG PFSS extrapolation example from the pfsspy docs](https://pfsspy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/auto_examples/using_pfsspy/plot_gong.html). Short version of this:

``` python
import astropy.constants as const
import astropy.units as u
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import sunpy.map
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord

import pfsspy
from pfsspy import coords, tracing
from pfsspy.sample_data import get_gong_map
gong_fname = get_gong_map()
gong_map = sunpy.map.Map(gong_fname)
nrho = 35
rss = 2.5
pfss_in = pfsspy.Input(gong_map, nrho, rss)
pfss_out = pfsspy.pfss(pfss_in)
tracer = tracing.PythonTracer()
r = 1.2 * const.R_sun
lat = np.linspace(-np.pi / 2, np.pi / 2, 8, endpoint=False)
lon = np.linspace(0, 2 * np.pi, 8, endpoint=False)
lat, lon = np.meshgrid(lat, lon, indexing='ij')
lat, lon = lat.ravel() * u.rad, lon.ravel() * u.rad
seeds = SkyCoord(lon, lat, r, frame=pfss_out.coordinate_frame)
field_lines = tracer.trace(seeds, pfss_out)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
KeyError                                  Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[9], line 1
----> 1 field_lines = tracer.trace(seeds, pfss_out)

It's based on some code I wrote earlier to convert the Carrington GONG map to Stonyhurst coordinates (in order to have the pfsspy output in Stonyhurst). But I'm not sure if it's stupid approach - and if it's sustainable. 

Because what I'm doing (see last two lines in the following example) is just to write a new header using `sunpy.map.header_helper.make_heliographic_header` and then reproject the GONG map to that header (without actually changing the coordinate system). Will this work in the future, or will it be removed because it's altering the header? And is it a bad idea to do it like this?

``` python 
import sunpy.map
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord

import pfsspy
from pfsspy import coords, tracing
from pfsspy.sample_data import get_gong_map
gong_fname = get_gong_map()
gong_map = sunpy.map.Map(gong_fname)

new_map_header = sunpy.map.header_helper.make_heliographic_header(date=gong_map.date, observer_coordinate=gong_map.observer_coordinate, shape=gong_map.data.shape, frame='carrington', projection_code='CEA')
gong_map = gong_map.reproject_to(new_map_header)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/1822910680/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

When reading cdf files with a lot of variables, for each variable `PerformanceWarning: DataFrame is highly fragmented.` is raised by Pandas, as each variable is added to a DataFrame using `df[var_key] = data`.

### To Reproduce

``` python
>>> # Importing sunpy_soar registers the client with sunpy
>>> import sunpy_soar
>>> from sunpy.net import Fido
>>> from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
>>> import sunpy.net.attrs as a
>>> 
>>> # Create search attributes
>>> time = a.Time('2022-02-01', '2022-02-01')
>>> level = a.Level(2)
>>> product = a.soar.Product('EPD-STEP-MAIN')
>>> 
>>> # Do search
>>> result = Fido.search(time & level & product)
>>> print(result)
>>> 
>>> # Download files
>>> files = Fido.fetch(result)
>>> print(files)
>>> 
>>> # Read files, using _read_cdf in the background
>>> solo_step = TimeSeries(files, concatenate=True)
  df[var_key + f'_{i}'] = col
/home/jagies/mambaforge/envs/dev_solo/lib/python3.9/site-packages/sunpy/io/_cdf.py:123: PerformanceWarning: DataFrame is highly fragmented.  This is usually the result of calling `frame.insert` many times, which has poor performance.  Consider joining all columns at once using pd.concat(axis=1) instead. To get a de-fragmented frame, use `newframe = frame.copy()`
  df[var_key] = data
/home/jagies/mambaforge/envs/dev_solo/lib/python3.9/site-packages/sunpy/io/_cdf.py:123: PerformanceWarning: DataFrame is highly fragmented.  This is usually the result of calling `frame.insert` many times, which has poor performance.  Consider joining all columns at once using pd.concat(axis=1) instead. To get a de-fragmented frame, use `newframe = frame.copy()`
  df[var_key] = data
/home/jagies/mambaforge/envs/dev_solo/lib/python3.9/site-packages/sunpy/io/_cdf.py:123: PerformanceWarning: DataFrame is highly fragmented.  This is usually the result of calling `frame.insert` many times, which has poor performance.  Consider joining all columns at once using pd.concat(axis=1) instead. To get a de-fragmented frame, use `newframe = frame.copy()`
  df[var_key] = data
WARNING: SunpyUserWarning: The variable "RTN_Pixels" has been skipped because it has more than 2 dimensions, which is unsupported. [sunpy.io._cdf]
```

### Proposed fix

According to some comments online (e.g., https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75766112/performancewarning-dataframe-is-highly-fragmented-when-creating-new-dataframe-c), it's memory wise more efficient to gather the data for all variables in a dictionary throughout the for-loop (over each variable), and in the end concat it to the desired DataFrame. 

I implemented those changes and will make a corresponding PR for it in a second. That gets rid of the PerformanceWarnings, and it seems to run a little bit faster on my machine, for the above example using `%timeit -n1 -r3` it's `7.69 s ± 10.8 ms per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 3 runs, 1 loop each)` vs. `6.79 s ± 67.5 ms per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 3 runs, 1 loop each)`. 

I don't have any official statement that this fix is really better or the way to go, but as it removes annoying warnings and seems to be not slower, maybe it's worthwhile to implement it.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7246/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7246/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

As described in #7246, when reading cdf files with a lot of variables, for each variable `PerformanceWarning: DataFrame is highly fragmented.` is raised by Pandas, as each variable is added to a DataFrame using `df[var_key] = data`.

This PR changes the function `sunpy.io._cdf.read_cdf` so that it gathers the data for all variables in a dictionary throughout the for-loop (over each variable), and in the end concat it to the desired DataFrame, which according to some comments online is memory wise more efficient.

This gets rid of the PerformanceWarnings, and it seems to run a little bit faster on my machine, for the above example using `%timeit -n1 -r3` it's `7.69 s ± 10.8 ms per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 3 runs, 1 loop each)` vs. `6.79 s ± 67.5 ms per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 3 runs, 1 loop each)`. 

I don't have any official statement that this fix is really better or the way to go, but as it removes annoying warnings and seems to be not slower, maybe it's worthwhile to implement The SOAR has updated and has changed all the product names to lowercase meaning that the products here dont work anymore (as all registered as capitalised).

There may be other changes too, I'll look into it now and update

### To Reproduce
``` python 
# Create search attributes
instrument = a.Instrument('EUI')
time = a.Time('2021-02-01', '2021-02-02')
level = a.Level(1)
product = a.soar.Product.eui_fsi304_image

# Do search
result = Fido.search(instrument & time & a.Level(1) & product)
print(result)
Results from 1 Provider:

0 Results from the SOARClient:

Instrument Data product Level Start time End time Filesize SOOP Name
                                                   Mbyte            
---------- ------------ ----- ---------- -------- -------- ---------

| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).

- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.

- [x] Build number is 0.

- [ ] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [ ] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

+{% set name = "streamtracer" %}
+{% set version = "2.0.1" %}
+
+package:
+  name: {{ name|lower }}
+  version: {{ version }}
+
+source:
+  url: https://pypi.io/packages/source/{{ name[0] }}/{{ name }}/streamtracer-{{ version }}.tar.gz
+  sha256: 29955653f88970ab3b1fe3e9c470e3b243c1a04e157647d22b2b13494f8864ca
+
+build:
+  script: {{ PYTHON }} -m pip install . -vv
+  number: 0
+  skip: true  # [py<37]
+
+requirements:
+  build:
+    - {{ compiler('c') }}
+    - {{ compiler('rust') }}
+  host:
+    - python
+    - setuptools >=45
+    - setuptools-scm >=6.2
+    - numpy
+    - maturin >=0.13,<0.14
+    - pip
+  run:
+    - python
+    - numpy >=1.14.5
+    - libgcc-ng  # [linux]
+
+test:
+  imports:
+    - streamtracer
+  commands:
+    - pip check
+  requires:
+    - pip
+
+about:
+  home: https://streamtracer.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

+{% set name = "streamtracer" %}
+{% set version = "2.0.1" %}
+

+  version: {{ version }}
+
+source:

+build:
+  script: {{ PYTHON }} -m pip install . -vv
+  number: 0
+  skip: true  # [py<37]
+

+    - {{ compiler('rust') }}

+    - python

+    - setuptools-scm >=6.2
+    - numpy
+    - maturin >=0.13,<0.14
+    - pip

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

+    - numpy

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7093","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7093/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7093/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7093/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7093","id":1789761978,"node_id":"I_kwDOACEKh85qrZm6","number":7093,"title":"Provide the option to skip specific variables to be loaded through TimeSeries","user":{"login":"jgieseler","id":39489154,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5NDg5MTU0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39489154?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler","html_url":"https://github.com/jgieseler","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-07-05T15:06:20Z","updated_at":"2023-07-05T15:06:20Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the feature

A bit related to #6424, I propose to proivde the option to skip specific variables to be loaded through TimeSeries.

This is (for me) motivated by the new in-situ instrumentation onboard Solar Orbiter and PSP, where we have 1s cadence and a lot of energy channels, resulting e.g. in memory problems when loading daily files of SolO/EPD/STEP data. You can try it yourself:

``` python
from sunpy.net import Fido
from sunpy.net import attrs as a
import sunpy_soar
from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
starttime="2022-1-26 10:30"
endtime="2022-1-27 15:40"
trange = a.Time(starttime, endtime)
product = a.soar.Product('EPD-STEP-MAIN')
res = Fido.search(a.Instrument('EPD') & trange & a.Level(2) & product)
files = Fido.fetch(res)
data = TimeSeries(files, concatenate=True)
```


### Proposed solution

In this case, one quick workaround I tested locally is to pass a list of cdf variables to be ignored to `read_cdf`, and then add
``` python
if var_key in ignore_vars:
    pass
else:
```
to the `for` loop at https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/ae900117b6afd8d81be17273b8a2e3ce1afc9732/sunpy/io/_cdf.py#L71

But this would only affect data loaded from cdf files. And it's a quite specific solution because you need to know what's in der cdf file and what you don't need (for the case of the SolO/EPD/STEP files one can omit all the countrates that are provided next to the fluxes).

I know that the TimeSeries gets an overhaul, so one might want to wait for this, but I wanted to mention it anyhow.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7093/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7093/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Package Details

- Package name: solarmach
- Maintainer(s): Jan Gieseler
- Repository: https://github.com/jgieseler/solarmach
- Documentation link: https://solarmach.readthedocs.io
- PyPI link (if applicable): https://pypi.org/project/solarmach
- Continuous integration link(s): https://github.com/jgieseler/solarmach/blob/main/.github/workflows/pytest.yml
- Link to Code of Conduct or page describing adopted Code of Conduct: https://github.com/jgieseler/solarmach/blob/main/code_of_conduct.md

## Description of Package
<!-- Roughly 1-3 sentences in length !-->
The Solar MAgnetic Connection HAUS tool (`solarmach`) is a Python package that derives (using `sunpy`) and visualizes the spatial configuration and solar magnetic connection of different observers in the heliosphere at different times. 

(Note: `solarmach` is the PyPI/conda-forge Python package that provides all functionalities to end users and to the `streamlit` web-app at https://solar-mach.github.io. The `streamlit` app itself is also open-source (cf. https://github.com/jgieseler/Solar-MACH), but not provided as a Python package.)


<!-- Submitters do not need to edit below this comment !-->

## Package Review

## Editor Submission Checklist

- [ ] Is the submission appropriate (compatible with the SunPy CoC)?
- [ ] Does the project have an [appropriate license](https://opensource.org/licenses/)?
- [ ] Is the project in an online version control system?
- [ ] Does the project provide a Python interface?
- [ ] Is the project on PyPI?
- [ ] Is the project useful to the solar physics community?
- [ ] Version that was reviewed (tag or commit hash)

## Instructions to Reviewer

Please copy the following and select the ranking for each criteria, the full review criteria can be found here:

```
* Functionality           : General Package / Specialized Package / Not Relevant
* Integration             : Well integrated / Partially Integrated / Minimal Integration
* Documentation           : Extensive / Some / Little
* Testing                 : Excellent / Good / Needs Work
* Duplication             : None / Some / Major
* Community               : Excellent / Good / Needs Work
* Development Status      : Stable / Subject to Change / Low Activity / Needs Work
```

Using sunpy 4.1.6 with the newest cdflib 1.0.2 results in `TypeError: 'CDFInfo' object is not subscriptable` when opening a CDF file with `TimeSeries()`.

### To Reproduce

>>> from sunpy.net import Fido
>>> from sunpy.net import attrs as a
>>> from sunpy.timeseries import TimeSeries
>>> import sunpy_soar
>>> 
>>> starttime="2022-1-26 10:30"
>>> endtime="2022-1-27 15:40"
>>> instrument = a.Instrument('EPD')
>>> trange = a.Time(starttime, endtime)
>>> level = a.Level(2)
>>> product = a.soar.Product('EPD-STEP-MAIN')
>>> results = Fido.search(instrument & trange & level & product)
>>> files = Fido.fetch(results)
>>> data = TimeSeries(files, concatenate=True)

File ~/anaconda3/envs/cdflib/lib/python3.9/site-packages/sunpy/io/cdf.py:39, in read_cdf(fname)
     37 cdf_info = cdf.cdf_info()
     38 meta = cdf.globalattsget()
---> 39 all_var_keys = cdf_info['rVariables'] + cdf_info['zVariables']
     40 var_attrs = {key: cdf.varattsget(key) for key in all_var_keys}
     41 # Get keys that depend on time

TypeError: 'CDFInfo' object is not subscriptable

Provide a general description of the issue or problem.

Large parts of `sunpy.io` are used only and written for `Map` and `Timeseries`, they are not meant to be used by users to open files.

These are:

1. JPEG2000
2. CDF
3. FITS (this is already done)
4. ASDF
5. File tools

The readers we do support for users are:
1. ANA
2. GENX
3. SRS 

These should not be private.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6373/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6373/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/1560704333","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6373#issuecomment-1560704333","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6373","id":1560704333,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh85dBnVN","user":{"login":"jgieseler","id":39489154,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5NDg5MTU0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39489154?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler","html_url":"https://github.com/jgieseler","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-24T08:47:40Z","updated_at":"2023-05-24T08:47:40Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I'm adding this here because it seems to be connected to this issue. In **sunpy 5.0.0rc1**, when trying to import (the in the future deprecated) `sunpy.io.read_cdf`, I get an _ModuleNotFoundError_, whereas I assume it should give a deprecation warning.

``` python
from sunpy.io.cdf import read_cdf
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ModuleNotFoundError                       Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[3], line 1
----> 1 from sunpy.io.cdf import read_cdf

File ~/anaconda3/envs/sunpy5rc/lib/python3.9/site-packages/sunpy/io/cdf.py:1
----> 1 from _cdf import *  # NOQA
      3 from sunpy.util.exceptions import warn_deprecated
      4 from . import _cdf

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named '_cdf'
```

The problem seems to be introduced by commit https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/commit/21a7b5ec5b58023ca17eadb0124cde233e0db6bf

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

If sunpy 4.1.5 is installed with Anaconda (`conda install -c conda-forge sunpy`), it seems to install a non-working version of h5py, causing an error when importing h5py:
```
ImportError: [..] /lib/python3.9/site-packages/h5py/defs.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so: undefined symbol: H5Pget_fapl_direct
```
The h5py version installed using the conda command from above is 3.7.0. As installing sunpy 4.1.5 with pip installs h5py 3.8.0, I manually updated it to 3.8.0 using conda, and that solves the problem for me (see example below). 

I don't know if the problem originates somewhere in the solver (maybe connected to something like this: https://github.com/h5py/h5py/issues/1880), and also don't know why h5py<3.8.0 doesn't work. A solution would be just to increase the required version, or find the actual problem.

### To Reproduce

$ conda create --name testsunpy python=3.9
$ conda activate testsunpy
$ conda install -c conda-forge sunpy
$ python
$ >>> from sunpy.io.cdf import read_cdf
```

At the moment, `read_cdf()` skips data columns that are >=3 dimensional data (cf. https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/5869 & https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/5975). I was wondering if there are some possibilities to load this data without too much effort because otherwise we're missing out some nice new spacecraft data.

### Proposed solution

The crucial part is at https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/main/sunpy/io/cdf.py#L92= where a column is skipped if its dimension is > 2. Would it be a possibility to rewrite it to reflect the dependencies in the column name, like it's done 3 lines below for dim = 2 data:

``` python
elif data.ndim == 2:
    # Multiple columns, give each column a unique label
    for i, col in enumerate(data.T):
        df[var_key + f'_{i}'] = col
        units[var_key + f'_{i}'] = unit
```

So for example, for the `var_key` ELECTRON_FLUX that has (next to EPOCH) an ENERGY and VIEWING dependency, something like "Electron_Flux_Energy1_Viewing1", "Electron_Flux_Energy1_Viewing2", "Electron_Flux_Energy2_Viewing1", and so on. Then we still have a standard DataFrame that is passed to GenericTimeSeries in [L105](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/main/sunpy/io/cdf.py#L105=)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6194/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/6194/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Describe the feature

At the moment, `read_cdf()` skips data columns that are >=3 dimensional data (cf. https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/5869 & https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/5975). I was wondering if there are some possibilities to load this data without too much effort because otherwise we're missing out some nice new spacecraft data.

### Proposed solution

The crucial part is at https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/main/sunpy/io/cdf.py#L92= where a column is skipped if its dimension is > 2. Would it be a possibility to rewrite it to reflect the dependencies in the column name, like it's done 3 lines below for dim = 2 data:

``` python
elif data.ndim == 2:
    # Multiple columns, give each column a unique label
    for i, col in enumerate(data.T):
        df[var_key + f'_{i}'] = col
        units[var_key + f'_{i}'] = unit
```

So for example, for the `var_key` ELECTRON_FLUX that has (next to EPOCH) an ENERGY and VIEWING dependency, something like "Electron_Flux_Energy1_Viewing1", "Electron_Flux_Energy1_Viewing2", "Electron_Flux_Energy2_Viewing1", and so on. Then we still have a standard DataFrame that is passed to GenericTimeSeries in [L105](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/main/sunpy/io/cdf.py#L105=)

Because in the past these in-situ particle measurements were what I would call 'pseudo' multidimensional data, where usually only `time` was a really 'free' dimension, whereas `energy` and `viewing` usually were confined to defined bins that don't change at all (like in the example @AthKouloumvakos showed for SolO/HET). But I think for PSP this really changed, and introducing an "easy solution" now might actually cause more problems than helping because people won't cross-check how the dependencies really are.

So in conclusion, I agree that we should either introduce a really thorough approach, or leave it as it's now.

@AthKouloumvakos, you should take a look at the PSP datasets I mentioned in https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/5869 because these have more dependencies than SolO/HET. But let's talk about that in person (virtually 😉).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/1148597869/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31","id":1238366683,"node_id":"I_kwDOFPQQCs5Jz_nb","number":31,"title":"Support for low latency (LL) data level","user":{"login":"jgieseler","id":39489154,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5NDg5MTU0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39489154?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler","html_url":"https://github.com/jgieseler","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-05-17T09:41:01Z","updated_at":"2022-05-17T09:41:01Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the feature

Because `sunpy_soar` interprets data `level` as an integer (e.g., `level = a.Level(1)`), it doesn't support low latency data. 

SOAR has multiple processing levels: L0, L1, L2, L3, LL01, LL02, LL03 (cf. http://soar.esac.esa.int/soar/#search or https://issues.cosmos.esa.int/solarorbiterwiki/display/SOSP/SOAR+%28Solar+Orbiter+Archive%29+-+How+to+Use#SOAR(SolarOrbiterArchive)HowtoUse-machine_interface). Right now, only the first 4 "normal" levels (L0, L1, L2, L3) are supported by `sunpy_soar`; those starting with LL (for low latency) not. Even though this data product is probably not demanded that much, I think it might be worthwhile to integrate this option, especially because it shouldn't be too complicated.

As far as I understand it, this would not only need changes in sunpy_soar/attrs.py: https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/blob/55549bc86287ba20b59906c1a608658c5212f596/sunpy_soar/attrs.py#L96-L99
But also somewhere in the search because it right now seems to omit low latency results. The following search provides 0 results, while on http://soar.esac.esa.int/soar/#search some LL02 data files are found:
``` python
>>> import sunpy_soar
>>> from sunpy.net import Fido
>>> from sunpy.net import attrs as a
>>> import datetime as dt

>>> time = a.Time(dt.date(2022, 3, 13), dt.date(2022, 3, 14))
>>> instrument = a.Instrument('MAG')
>>> Fido.search(instrument & time)
<sunpy.net.fido_factory.UnifiedResponse object at 0x7f530c549ab0>
Results from 1 Provider:

0 Results from the SOARClient:

Instrument Data product Level Start time End time Filesize
                                                   Mbyte  
---------- ------------ ----- ---------- -------- --------

```




### Proposed solution

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31","id":1238366683,"node_id":"I_kwDOFPQQCs5Jz_nb","number":31,"title":"Support for low latency (LL) data level","user":{"login":"jgieseler","id":39489154,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5NDg5MTU0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39489154?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler","html_url":"https://github.com/jgieseler","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":2856162101,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyODU2MTYyMTAx","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/labels/enhancement","name":"enhancement","color":"a2eeef","default":true,"description":"New feature or request"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2022-05-17T09:41:01Z","updated_at":"2022-05-17T12:48:35Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the feature

Because `sunpy_soar` interprets data `level` as an integer (e.g., `level = a.Level(1)`), it doesn't support low latency data. 

SOAR has multiple processing levels: L0, L1, L2, L3, LL01, LL02, LL03 (cf. http://soar.esac.esa.int/soar/#search or https://issues.cosmos.esa.int/solarorbiterwiki/display/SOSP/SOAR+%28Solar+Orbiter+Archive%29+-+How+to+Use#SOAR(SolarOrbiterArchive)HowtoUse-machine_interface). Right now, only the first 4 "normal" levels (L0, L1, L2, L3) are supported by `sunpy_soar`; those starting with LL (for low latency) not. Even though this data product is probably not demanded that much, I think it might be worthwhile to integrate this option, especially because it shouldn't be too complicated.


### Proposed solution

As far as I understand it, this would not only need changes in sunpy_soar/attrs.py: https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/blob/55549bc86287ba20b59906c1a608658c5212f596/sunpy_soar/attrs.py#L96-L99
But also somewhere in the search because it right now seems to omit low latency results. The following search provides 0 results, while on http://soar.esac.esa.int/soar/#search some LL02 data files are found:
``` python
>>> import sunpy_soar
>>> from sunpy.net import Fido
>>> from sunpy.net import attrs as a
>>> import datetime as dt

>>> time = a.Time(dt.date(2022, 3, 13), dt.date(2022, 3, 14))
>>> instrument = a.Instrument('MAG')
>>> Fido.search(instrument & time)
<sunpy.net.fido_factory.UnifiedResponse object at 0x7f530c549ab0>
Results from 1 Provider:

0 Results from the SOARClient:

Instrument Data product Level Start time End time Filesize
                                                   Mbyte  
---------- ------------ ----- ---------- -------- --------

```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/comments/1128827275","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31#issuecomment-1128827275","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/31","id":1128827275,"node_id":"IC_kwDOFPQQCs5DSImL","user":{"login":"jgieseler","id":39489154,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5NDg5MTU0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39489154?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler","html_url":"https://github.com/jgieseler","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jgieseler/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-05-17T12:48:35Z","updated_at":"2022-05-17T12:48:35Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"That sounds reasonable. :+1: ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy-soar/issues/comments/1128827275/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
